verdict_reason: P1 briefing-pack issues would cause evaluators to run impossible or mismatched tests: missing app entrypoint, unspecified upload fixtures, F3 upload limits inconsistent with substrate, and F15 ZIP expectations inconsistent with the Phase 1 JSON export substrate.

findings.description: The evaluator prerequisites say only that the backend is running locally or on a dev cluster, but the workflow never gives the actual POLARIS URL, route, or auth context to open. A non-developer evaluator cannot start the walkthrough from this pack alone.

The upload block requires a small PDF, a 50MB PDF, an image-only PDF, and a document-specific query, but the pack supplies no fixture paths, sample files, or expected document content. This prevents consistent F3 observation capture across evaluators.

Input #12 expects a 50MB PDF to complete, and briefing.md line 41 says PDFs up to 50MB are accepted, but the Phase 1 upload substrate enforces a 25 MB limit in src/polaris_v6/api/upload.py. This would make the documented test fail even when the implementation matches its accepted substrate.

The F15 flow expects a ZIP that can be unzipped into report.md, evidence/, trace.jsonl, and provenance.json, but task_1_6's accepted substrate is src/polaris_v6/api/bundle.py plus downloadBundleAsJson, returning an EvidenceContract JSON export. The briefing and corpus would mis-grade the shipped Phase 1 export behavior.

The BPEI example lists Beth Israel Deaconess as a candidate meaning, and recording_template.md line 19 repeats it. Public references identify BPEI as Bascom Palmer Eye Institute, not Beth Israel Deaconess: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bascom_Palmer_Eye_Institute.

The briefing says evaluators will review 3 features, but the table lists four feature rows: F1, F2, F3a/F3b, and F15. Line 6 also includes F4 in scope even though the pack does not orient the evaluator to a distinct F4 test.
